screenshot_note: Puppeteer available but remote site unreachable (socket hang up on navigation) — code review only
theme: Six bugs killed in one sprint; two stubborn holdouts enter their fifth review; three fresh findings arrive
visual_observations: activityByWorker (App.jsx:959) NOW says `.slice(-5)`. WorkerCard (App.jsx:312) also says `.slice(-5)`. The mismatch that silently capped card history at 3 entries is gone.

handleCharClick (Office.jsx:1259) NOW guards with `worker.id !== 'bloberto'` before calling onWorkerClick. Clicking Bloberto no longer ghost-navigates to the dashboard. Fixed after four consecutive reviews.
wb-task-count (Office.jsx:1395) NOW reads `fontSize: '0.6rem'`. Raised from the 0.4rem sub-pixel value. Fixed after four reviews.
Wandering workers (Office.jsx:1595) NOW uses `meetingWorkerIds.has(w.id)`. The long-standing reference-equality bug is gone. Fixed after four reviews.
WallClock (Office.jsx:1131) NOW has `pointerEvents: 'none'`. The invisible click trap is disarmed.

Conference table render blocks (Office.jsx:1021, 1039) NOW both declare `const cx = 52, cy = 52, orbitR = 38` and compute `const y = cy - orbitR * Math.cos(rad)`. The coincidental-correctness variable shadow is gone.

VibeSparkline (App.jsx:426-433) — review #88 flagged 'double-announce' but the SVG root has NO <title> child element; the <title> tags are inside individual <circle> children (line 430). This is normal SVG tooltip behavior on data points, not a double-announcement pattern. Finding was inaccurate — closing.
workerConfetti (App.jsx:1172) STILL `Array.from({ length: 30 }, ...)` inline allocation. CONFETTI_PIECES at line 35 is module-scope. Fix exists one function above. Fifth consecutive review.
quoteBonus (App.jsx:1449) STILL `p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? 1 : p + 1` — wraps silently. Counter disappears at the boundary with no ceremony. Fifth consecutive review.
lone-survivor-label (Office.jsx:1588) `style={{ fontSize: '0.45rem' ... }}` — ~7.2px. The adjacent wb-task-count was raised to 0.6rem last review; this label was untouched. NEW finding.

WorkerCard (App.jsx:312) `activityEntries.filter(e => e.worker === worker.name)` — activityEntries is already pre-filtered by worker name via activityByWorker.get(worker.name) (line 959). The second filter is redundant and allocates a new array on every render. NEW finding.

WindowElement (Office.jsx:873-874) declares `const isNight = ...` then immediately `const isMidnight = isNight`. The two variables are identical — isMidnight is a dead alias. Every downstream usage of `isMidnight` could use `isNight` directly. NEW finding.

fixed_since_last_review.title: activityByWorker Slices to 3 But WorkerCard Expects 5 — Fixed
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx:959 — changed `.slice(-3)` to `.slice(-5)`. The pre-filtered map and the card's consumer now agree on depth.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Bloberto Click Ghost-Navigates to Dashboard — Fixed After Four Reviews
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx:1259 — added `worker.id !== 'bloberto'` guard before calling onWorkerClick. Clicking Bloberto still shows a ping reaction but no longer silently navigates away.

fixed_since_last_review.title: wb-task-count at 0.4rem Is Below Legibility Threshold — Fixed After Four Reviews
Wandering Workers .includes() Object Reference — Fixed After Four Reviews
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx:1595 — changed to `idleWorkers.filter(w => !meetingWorkerIds.has(w.id))`. The Set is now used as intended.

fixed_since_last_review.title: WallClock pointerEvents:auto With No Handler — Fixed
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx:1131 — changed `pointerEvents: 'auto'` to `pointerEvents: 'none'`. The clock is correctly marked as decorative; clicks pass through.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Conference Table Seated-Worker y Coordinate Uses cx Instead of cy — Fixed
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx:1021 and 1039 — both blocks now declare `const cx = 52, cy = 52, orbitR = 38` and compute `y = cy - orbitR * Math.cos(rad)`. The coincidental-correctness shadow is gone.

fixed_since_last_review.title: VibeSparkline Double-Announce — Finding Was Inaccurate, Closing
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Review #88 claimed a <title> existed as a direct child of the <svg> root alongside aria-label. Code inspection shows no such element — the <title> tags are inside individual <circle> children (SVG tooltip pattern, not a double-announce). Finding is closed as not reproducible.

ideas.title: lone-survivor-label at 0.45rem Is Below Legibility Threshold — New Finding
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1588 — `style={{ fontSize: '0.45rem', opacity: 0.6, ... }}` renders at ~7.2px. The wb-task-count fix in review #88 raised that label from 0.4rem to 0.6rem. The lone-survivor-label sits right next to it in the same character render block and wasn't touched. Fix: change `0.45rem` to `0.6rem` for consistency with the wb-task-count fix. WHY: information rendered below 10px is worse than absent — it implies a broken UI, and the fix is one character.

ideas.title: WorkerCard Redundant Filter Creates Array On Every Render — New Finding
ideas.description: App.jsx:312 — `const workerHistory = activityEntries.filter(e => e.worker === worker.name).slice(-5).reverse()`. The `activityEntries` prop is set from `activityByWorker.get(worker.name)` (App.jsx:1317) — already filtered to only that worker's entries. The `.filter(e => e.worker === worker.name)` on line 312 is a no-op that allocates a new array on every render of every WorkerCard. Fix: remove the redundant `.filter(...)` call and use `activityEntries.slice(-5).reverse()` directly. WHY: every card flip triggers a rerender; eliminating the dead allocation is a clean win. Also, the two-level truncation pattern (filter + slice) will confuse future maintainers who may adjust one without the other.

ideas.title: WindowElement isMidnight Is a Dead Alias for isNight — New Finding
ideas.description: Office.jsx:873-874 — `const isNight = (hour >= 0 && hour <= 4) || hour >= 20` then `const isMidnight = isNight`. The two variables are identical. `isMidnight` is used at line 883 to conditionally render the star field, but calling it 'midnight' while the range covers 8 PM to 4 AM is misleading. Fix option A (cleanup): delete `isMidnight` and replace its single use with `isNight`. Fix option B (correct semantics): change `isMidnight` to check only `hour >= 0 && hour <= 4` — the actual midnight window — and let `isNight` handle the full 8 PM-4 AM range. Option B creates a better starfield that only appears at true midnight, which would make the window more expressive. WHY: dead aliases are traps for future authors; naming a thing wrong makes every reader re-derive its meaning.

ideas.title: workerConfetti Inline Allocation — Fifth Review Flagging, Fix Pattern Exists
ideas.description: App.jsx:1172 — `Array.from({ length: 30 }, (_, i) => (<span .../>))` allocated inline in JSX every render during the 2500ms confetti window. CONFETTI_PIECES at line 35 is the same pattern at module scope and is used for vibe-rank confetti. The difference: the worker confetti version applies a worker color to the first 18 pieces via `style={{ '--i': i, ...(i < 18 ? { background: workerConfetti.color } : {}) }}`. Fix: create `WORKER_CONFETTI_PIECES` at module scope that applies only `--i`, then override the color via a CSS custom property on the parent `.confetti-layer` div: `style={{ '--worker-color': workerConfetti.color }}`, and let the first 18 `.confetti-piece` variants pick it up. WHY: fifth consecutive review. The fix is 8 lines.

ideas.title: quoteBonus Completion Has No Ceremony — Fifth Review Flagging
ideas.description: App.jsx:1449 — `p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? 1 : p + 1` wraps silently. The counter at the end of the line (`{quoteBonus > 0 && quoteBonus < BONUS_TAGLINES.length && {quoteBonus}/{BONUS_TAGLINES.length}}`) disappears at the boundary with no acknowledgment. Fix: when `p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length`, before resetting to 1, fire a `completionToast({ text: '✦ You found all the thoughts. Bloberto is impressed.', type: 'info' })` with golden border styling (`border: '2px solid #fbbf24'`), and set a localStorage flag. After the flag is set, render a permanent `✦` after the VIBE_VERSIONS span as a badge of completion. WHY: fifth consecutive review. The tagline hunt is the only discovery mechanic in the footer. Its ending must land.

ideas.title: Ghost Desk 'Last Active' Tooltip Lacks Human Time
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1476 — vacant/ghost desks show `📋 Vacant` or `↪ away` nameplates. Ghost workers (roster members not currently active) have a `updated_at` field available. The hover card for ghost characters (Office.jsx:823-843) shows role and task but no last-seen time. Fix: on the ghost desk nameplate, append a relative time: `↪ {worker.name.split(' ')[0]} · {getRelativeTime(worker.updated_at)}`. WHY: 'last seen 2 hours ago' makes the absence feel real — a person stepped away, not a placeholder. Idle desks tell a story. The `getRelativeTime` utility is already imported in Office.jsx.

ideas.title: After-Hours Shift Summary Banner
ideas.description: When `vibe === 'after-hours'` and workers transition out (fadingMap has entries), there's no daily wrap-up moment. Idea: compute a 'shift summary' from activityLog when the after-hours vibe activates: count completions, errors, and longest task of the day. Show it as a toast styled like a slack EOD message: '📦 Day summary: 4 shipped · 1 error · longest task: 3h 20m.' WHY: the end of the work day is a natural emotional beat. Acknowledging it transforms the office from a status board into a place with a narrative arc. Data is already available in activityLog.

ideas.title: Tag Filter State Persists Across Tab Switches
ideas.description: App.jsx:746 — `selectedTag` state survives tab switches. If someone activates a tag filter on the dashboard, switches to the office, then back, the dimming is still active. This is probably intentional, but there's no visual affordance on the tab itself indicating a filter is active. Fix: when `selectedTag !== null`, add a subtle indicator dot or '·' to the Dashboard tab button (`2 Dashboard ●`). WHY: invisible application state causes confusion — users don't know why cards are dimmed after returning from the office view.

ideas.title: Bloberto's Mood Board: The Manager's Desk Has a Story
ideas.description: Wild idea: clicking the manager desk nameplate (not Bloberto himself — the nameplate beneath his desk that shows his vibe title like 'LEGEND 🔥' or 'CVO') opens a 'Bloberto's Board' overlay. The board is a tiny corkboard rendered in SVG showing: (1) a pinned sticky note with today's date and a procedurally-generated one-liner based on vibe + worker count (e.g., 'Q3 looking fine. Everyone is here. I'm not panicking.'), (2) a bar chart made of emoji showing the day's vibe history from vibeHistory, (3) a fake TODO list with two items checked off and one perpetually unchecked ('fix the build' — always unchecked). The board uses the existing nameplate ref and slides in from behind the desk. WHY: the manager character is the most prominent figure in the office but the least interactive. Giving Bloberto a private board surfaces the analytics in a way that feels human, not dashboardy. Uses existing data — no new APIs needed.

ideas.title: Office Hours Mode: The Team Clock Out Ritual
ideas.description: Wild idea: at 5 PM local time (or when vibe transitions to 'after-hours'), animate all working characters doing a 'pack up' sequence — their desk screens fade to a screensaver state, their character briefly shows a wave emoji above their head, and they walk toward the door one by one with a 600ms stagger. The door pulses once. A banner reads 'See you tomorrow.' After the animation, the office shows only ghost desks and the after-hours sky. No new data needed — the ritual triggers purely on time/vibe transition. WHY: logging off should feel like something. The office currently snaps from full to empty. A 3-second goodbye ritual would make the whole day feel worthwhile.
